outcome_narration.description: What both players are told happened, given the resolved actions and any dice/combat results provided to you. Narrate combat numbers you were given (HP lost, sever, stagger) -- never invent your own damage numbers or decide success/failure yourself.

character.description: A player character's name OR the current monster's name if one is active.

hp_changes.description: For combat turns, this should match the effective_damage/hp_after numbers the orchestrator already computed and gave you in this turn's context -- do not recompute or invent your own.

flags_set.description: Free-text world-state flags other systems can key off later, e.g. 'third_chalice_prepared'.

description.description: What both players now know about this NPC publicly. Do not include GM-only agenda/leverage details from hidden_state.json here.

npcs_revealed.description: Use when a hidden_state.json hidden_npc becomes known to the players this turn.

limb.description: head/torso/arm_left/arm_right/leg_left/leg_right for a PC, head/torso/arm/leg for a monster.

limb_effects.description: Section 10. Only set this from a stagger_crossed=true or sever=true result the orchestrator already gave you this turn -- never decide a sever/stagger yourself.

status_track_changes.description: Section 5. Building Blood Loss/Influence/Corrosion from a hit or hazard is a narrative-judgment call you make (how many stacks a given hit/moment warrants); the ruleset's per-stack math (e.g. Blood Loss bonus damage) is handled by the orchestrator once you set the delta.

insight_changes.description: Section 11. Usually +1 per discovery trigger (first sighting of a new archetype, lore, surviving a near-death, defeating a boss) -- once per discovery.

archetype.description: Section 16 default archetype table. The orchestrator generates the actual HP/stats from this -- you only pick which archetype fits the fiction.

name.description: A fiction-appropriate name/description for this specific instance.

monster_spawned.description: Set this when this turn's events introduce a new hostile encounter. Only one monster can be active at a time in this simulation -- don't set this if one is already active (check the encounter state you were given in context).

encounter_ended.description: Set true when the active monster is defeated, flees, or the encounter otherwise resolves -- clears the encounter so a new one can spawn later.

saves_triggered.description: Section 5 Save Roll. Flag when this turn's events call for one (taking damage, witnessing something wrong, a fear moment, an Influence source acting). The orchestrator rolls it using the CURRENT track value and surfaces the result at the top of next turn's scene -- you will not know the outcome this turn.

triggers_fired.description: IDs of hidden_state.json triggers that fired this turn, if any.

gm_private_notes.description: Never shown to players. Running GM read on tension, pending consequences, promises made.
